description:
The delivery plugin's cwd-safety guard refused teardown when the invoker's $PWD was inside the code worktree, to stop an agent deleting its own cwd. That underlying wedging bug is FIXED, so the control is removed entirely (it was always best-effort $PWD anyway — §11 'not a core guarantee').

REMOVE:
- src/delivery.rs: guard_cwd() fn + its two calls in dispatch (close.pre, close|unclaim|drop.post); the Spec.cwd field; related doc comments. Dispatch arms simplify to bare deliver/release.
- src/bin/bl-delivery.rs: the $PWD capture (env::var_os(PWD)) and cwd: in both Spec constructions. KEEP env::current_dir() — that feeds resolve_id/changed_task_paths, unrelated.
- src/delivery_tests.rs + tests/delivery.rs: the guard tests (every_*_refuses_when_invoked_from_inside_the_worktree, an_equal_cwd_refuses...); drop cwd from the spec() helper.
- docs/architecture.md §11 (lines ~654-655): delete the '+ best-effort cwd-safety guard...never close from inside the worktree' parenthetical, leaving 'close.post teardown'.

SOFTEN (do not remove):
- SKILL.md: the 'inviolable rule / binary will refuse (cannot close from within the worktree)' (lines ~9,27,460) becomes an ADVISORY recommendation: cd out of the worktree before close so your shell's cwd isn't deleted under you; balls no longer enforces it.

This reverts bl-4d00 (cwd-guard on all teardown) and the pre-existing close-only guard. 100% coverage + line-length gate; full suite must pass.
title: Remove the delivery cwd-guard control (guarded bug is fixed) + soften skill doc to advisory
